panels.description:
Tickets Available Read displays the number of read tickets available to the WiredTiger storage engine. Read tickets represent the number of concurrent read operations allowed into the storage engine. When this value reaches zero, new read requests might queue until a read ticket becomes available.

Tickets Available Write displays the number of write tickets available to the WiredTiger storage engine. Write tickets represent the number of concurrent write operations allowed into the storage engine. When this value reaches zero, new write requests might queue until a write ticket becomes available.

targets.query:
from(bucket: v.defaultBucket)
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "ftdc")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["hostname"] =~ /^${hostname:regex}$/)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["version"] =~ /^${version:regex}$/)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "serverStatus.wiredTiger.concurrentTransactions.write.available" 
                    or r["_field"] == "serverStatus.wiredTiger.concurrentTransactions.read.available"
                    or r["_field"] == "serverStatus.globalLock.activeClients.writers"
                    or r["_field"] == "serverStatus.globalLock.activeClients.readers")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({r with _field: 
    if r._field == "serverStatus.wiredTiger.concurrentTransactions.write.available" then "write tickets avail"
    else if r._field == "serverStatus.wiredTiger.concurrentTransactions.read.available" then "read tickets avail"
    else if r._field == "serverStatus.globalLock.activeClients.writers" then "active writers"
    else if r._field == "serverStatus.globalLock.activeClients.readers" then "active readers"
    else r._field
  }))
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: last, createEmpty: false)

panels.title: WiredTiger Tickets / Active Clients

panels.description:
used displays the total bytes cached in memory for serving reads and writes.

These metrics include both indexes and data from the working set.

Sustained high cache usage indicates the RAM is too small for your workloads. Optimize your queries to avoid frequent disk reads. If write operations make cache usage high, throttle them.

targets.query:
from(bucket: v.defaultBucket)
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "ftdc")
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r["hostname"] =~ /^${hostname:regex}$/)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r["version"] =~ /^${version:regex}$/)
 |> filter(fn: (r) =>  r["_field"] == "serverStatus.wiredTiger.cache.maximum bytes configured"  or r["_field"] == "serverStatus.wiredTiger.cache.bytes currently in the cache" )
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ r with _value: r._value / 1024 / 1024 / 1024}))
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({r with _field:
    if r._field == "serverStatus.wiredTiger.cache.maximum bytes configured" then "max configured"
    else if r._field == "serverStatus.wiredTiger.cache.bytes currently in the cache" then "in cache"
    else r._field
  }))
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: last, createEmpty: false)

panels.title: WiredTiger cache

panels.description:
read Into displays the rate in bytes per second of data read from disk into memory to service queries.

written From  displays the rate in bytes per second of data written from memory into disk to service writes.

targets.query:
from(bucket: v.defaultBucket)
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "ftdc")
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r["hostname"] =~ /^${hostname:regex}$/)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r["version"] =~ /^${version:regex}$/)
 |> filter(fn: (r) =>  r["_field"] == "serverStatus.wiredTiger.cache.bytes written from cache" or r["_field"] == "serverStatus.wiredTiger.cache.bytes read into cache")
  |> derivative(unit: 1s)
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({r with _field:
    if r._field == "serverStatus.wiredTiger.cache.bytes written from cache" then "written from cache"
    else if r._field == "serverStatus.wiredTiger.cache.bytes read into cache" then "read into cache"
    else r._field
  }))
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: last, createEmpty: false)

panels.title: Bytes read into / written from cache

panels.description:
Clean vs dirty data

Clean refers to data in the cache that is identical to the version stored on disk.
Dirty refers to data in the cache that has been modified and needs to be reconciled with the data stored on the disk.

Clean vs dirty eviction

In the case of clean eviction, there is no dirty content on the page and it is directly removed from the memory. After that, the page remains solely on the disk unchanged.
In the case of dirty eviction, the dirty page goes through reconciliation. The obsolete content on the page is thrown out, the latest value goes to the data store and older values go to the history store.

targets.query:
from(bucket: v.defaultBucket)
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "ftdc")
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r["hostname"] =~ /^${hostname:regex}$/)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r["version"] =~ /^${version:regex}$/)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "serverStatus.wiredTiger.cache.modified pages evicted" or r["_field"] == "serverStatus.wiredTiger.cache.unmodified pages evicted")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ r with _value: r._value }))
  |> derivative(unit: 1s)
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({r with _field:
    if r._field == "serverStatus.wiredTiger.cache.modified pages evicted" then "modified"
    else if r._field == "serverStatus.wiredTiger.cache.unmodified pages evicted" then "unmodified"
    else r._field
  }))
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: last, createEmpty: false)

panels.title: Pages Evicted

panels.description: indicates modified data that needs to be written to the data files via a checkpoint.

names: serverStatus.wiredTiger.cache.tracked dirty bytes in the cache

panels.description: A checkpoint is a known point in time from which WiredTiger can recover in the event of a crash or unexpected shutdown.

 |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "serverStatus.wiredTiger.block-manager.bytes written for checkpoint")

panels.description: The WiredTiger block manager subsystem manages the reading and writing of data from the disk. It is designed to facilitate high performance, economic use of disk space and customizability.

panels.title: wiredTiger Block Manager

panels.description:
A WiredTiger Data Handle (dhandle) is a generic representation of any named data source. This representation contains information such as its name, how many references there are to it, individual data source statistics and what type of underlying data object it is.

WiredTiger maintains all dhandles in a global dhandle list accessed from the connection. Multiple sessions access this list, which is protected by a R/W lock. Each session also maintains a session dhandle cache which is a cache of dhandles a session has operated upon. The entries in the cache are references into the global dhandle list.

targets.query:
from(bucket: v.defaultBucket)
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)

 |> filter(fn: (r) => r["version"] =~ /^${version:regex}$/)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "serverStatus.wiredTiger.data-handle.connection data handles currently active")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({r with _field: "active data handles"}))
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: last, createEmpty: false)
  |> yield(name: "last")

targets.query:

    if r._field == "serverStatus.connections.active" then "active"
    else if r._field == "serverStatus.connections.current" then "current"
    else if r._field == "serverStatus.connections.available" then "available"

from(bucket: v.defaultBucket)

 |> filter(fn: (r) => r["hostname"] =~ /^${hostname:regex}$/)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r["version"] =~ /^${version:regex}$/)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => 
      r["_field"] == "serverStatus.opcounters.update" or
      r["_field"] == "serverStatus.opcounters.query" or
      r["_field"] == "serverStatus.opcounters.insert" or
      r["_field"] == "serverStatus.opcounters.getmore" or
      r["_field"] == "serverStatus.opcounters.delete" or
      r["_field"] == "serverStatus.opcounters.command")
  |> derivative(unit: 1s, nonNegative: true)
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({r with _field:
    if r._field == "serverStatus.opcounters.update" then "update"
    else if r._field == "serverStatus.opcounters.query" then "query"
    else if r._field == "serverStatus.opcounters.insert" then "insert"
    else if r._field == "serverStatus.opcounters.getmore" then "getmore"
    else if r._field == "serverStatus.opcounters.delete" then "delete"
    else if r._field == "serverStatus.opcounters.command" then "command"
    else r._field
  }))
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: last, createEmpty: false)

from(bucket: v.defaultBucket)
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "ftdc")
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r["hostname"] =~ /^${hostname:regex}$/)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r["version"] =~ /^${version:regex}$/)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] =~ /replSetGetStatus\.members\.\d+\.lastAppliedWallTime|serverStatus\.localTime/)
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: last, createEmpty: false)
  |> pivot(rowKey:["_time"], columnKey: ["_field"], valueColumn: "_value")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({
      _time: r._time,
      member_0: if exists r["replSetGetStatus.members.0.lastAppliedWallTime"] then (float(v: r["serverStatus.localTime"]) - float(v: r["replSetGetStatus.members.0.lastAppliedWallTime"])) / 1000.0 else 0.0,
      member_1: if exists r["replSetGetStatus.members.1.lastAppliedWallTime"] then (float(v: r["serverStatus.localTime"]) - float(v: r["replSetGetStatus.members.1.lastAppliedWallTime"])) / 1000.0 else 0.0,
      member_2: if exists r["replSetGetStatus.members.2.lastAppliedWallTime"] then (float(v: r["serverStatus.localTime"]) - float(v: r["replSetGetStatus.members.2.lastAppliedWallTime"])) / 1000.0 else 0.0,
      member_3: if exists r["replSetGetStatus.members.3.lastAppliedWallTime"] then (float(v: r["serverStatus.localTime"]) - float(v: r["replSetGetStatus.members.3.lastAppliedWallTime"])) / 1000.0 else 0.0,      // Add more members as needed
  }))
  |> yield(name: "differences")

 |> filter(fn: (r) => r["hostname"] =~ /^${hostname:regex}$/)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r["version"] =~ /^${version:regex}$/)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "local.oplog.rs.stats.storageStats.avgObjSize")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({r with _field: "avgObjSize"}))
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)

from(bucket: v.defaultBucket)
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "ftdc")
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r["hostname"] =~ /^${hostname:regex}$/)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r["version"] =~ /^${version:regex}$/)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "serverStatus.flowControl.isLaggedTimeMicros")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({r with _field: "isLaggedTimeMicros"}))
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: last, createEmpty: false)
  |> derivative(unit: v.windowPeriod)
  |> yield(name: "last")

from(bucket: v.defaultBucket)
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "ftdc")
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r["hostname"] =~ /^${hostname:regex}$/)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r["version"] =~ /^${version:regex}$/)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "serverStatus.asserts.warning" or r["_field"] == "serverStatus.asserts.user" or r["_field"] == "serverStatus.asserts.rollovers" or r["_field"] == "serverStatus.asserts.regular" or r["_field"] == "serverStatus.asserts.msg")
  |> derivative(unit: v.windowPeriod)
    |> map(fn: (r) => ({r with _field:
    if r._field == "serverStatus.asserts.warning" then "warning"
    else if r._field == "serverStatus.asserts.user" then "user"
    else if r._field == "serverStatus.asserts.rollovers" then "rollovers"
    else if r._field == "serverStatus.asserts.regular" then "regular"
    else if r._field == "serverStatus.asserts.msg" then "msg"
    else r._field
  }))
    |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)

  |> derivative(unit: v.windowPeriod)
    |> map(fn: (r) => ({r with _field:
    if r._field == "serverStatus.opcountersRepl.command" then "command"
    else if r._field == "serverStatus.opcountersRepl.delete" then "delete"
    else if r._field == "serverStatus.opcountersRepl.getmore" then "getmore"
    else if r._field == "serverStatus.opcountersRepl.insert" then "insert"
    else if r._field == "serverStatus.opcountersRepl.query" then "query"
    else if r._field == "serverStatus.opcountersRepl.update" then "update"
    else r._field
  }))
    |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)

from(bucket: v.defaultBucket)

|> derivative(unit: v.windowPeriod)
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({r with _field:
    if r._field == "serverStatus.metrics.commands.createIndexes.total" then "createIndexes"
    else if r._field == "serverStatus.metrics.commands.dataSize.total" then "dataSize"
    else if r._field == "serverStatus.metrics.commands.dropIndexes.total" then "dropIndexes"
    else if r._field == "serverStatus.metrics.commands.listCollections.total" then "listCollections"

 |> filter(fn: (r) => r["hostname"] =~ /^${hostname:regex}$/)

  |> map(fn: (r) => ({r with _field:
    if r._field == "serverStatus.metrics.cursor.open.noTimeout" then "open.noTimeout"
    else if r._field == "serverStatus.metrics.cursor.open.pinned" then "open.pinned"
    else if r._field == "serverStatus.metrics.cursor.open.total" then "open.total"
    else if r._field == "serverStatus.metrics.cursor.timedOut" then "timedOut"
    else r._field

  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: last, createEmpty: false)

from(bucket: v.defaultBucket)
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "ftdc")
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r["hostname"] =~ /^${hostname:regex}$/)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r["version"] =~ /^${version:regex}$/)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "serverStatus.metrics.document.updated" or r["_field"] == "serverStatus.metrics.document.returned" or r["_field"] == "serverStatus.metrics.document.inserted" or r["_field"] == "serverStatus.metrics.document.deleted")
  |> derivative(unit: v.windowPeriod)
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({r with _field:
    if r._field == "serverStatus.metrics.document.updated" then "updated"
    else if r._field == "serverStatus.metrics.document.returned" then "returned"
    else if r._field == "serverStatus.metrics.document.inserted" then "inserted"
    else if r._field == "serverStatus.metrics.document.deleted" then "deleted"
    else r._field
  }))
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)

from(bucket: v.defaultBucket)
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "ftdc")
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r["hostname"] =~ /^${hostname:regex}$/)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r["version"] =~ /^${version:regex}$/)
 |> filter(fn: (r) =>
      r["_field"] == "serverStatus.metrics.document.returned" or 
      r["_field"] == "serverStatus.metrics.queryExecutor.scannedObjects")
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)
  |> pivot(
      rowKey: ["_time"],
      columnKey: ["_field"],
      valueColumn: "_value"
  )
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({
      _time: r._time,
      scanned_returned_ratio: r["serverStatus.metrics.queryExecutor.scannedObjects"] / r["serverStatus.metrics.document.returned"]
  }))
  |> yield(name: "scanned_returned_ratio")

from(bucket: v.defaultBucket)
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "ftdc")
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r["hostname"] =~ /^${hostname:regex}$/)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r["version"] =~ /^${version:regex}$/)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "serverStatus.network.physicalBytesIn" or r["_field"] == "serverStatus.network.physicalBytesOut")
  |> derivative(unit: 1s)
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({r with _field:
    if r._field == "serverStatus.network.physicalBytesIn" then "physicalBytesIn"
    else if r._field == "serverStatus.network.physicalBytesOut" then "physicalBytesOut"
    else r._field
  }))
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: last, createEmpty: false)

from(bucket: v.defaultBucket)
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "ftdc")
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r["hostname"] =~ /^${hostname:regex}$/)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r["version"] =~ /^${version:regex}$/)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "serverStatus.network.bytesOut" or r["_field"] == "serverStatus.network.bytesIn")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ r with _value: r._value / 1024 /1024 }))
  |> derivative(unit: 1s)
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({r with _field:
    if r._field == "serverStatus.network.bytesOut" then "bytesOut"
    else if r._field == "serverStatus.network.bytesIn" then "bytesIn"
    else r._field
  }))
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: last, createEmpty: false)

from(bucket: v.defaultBucket)
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "ftdc")
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r["hostname"] =~ /^${hostname:regex}$/)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r["version"] =~ /^${version:regex}$/)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "local.oplog.rs.stats.storageStats.freeStorageSize" or r["_field"] == "local.oplog.rs.stats.storageStats.storageSize")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({r with _field:
    if r._field == "local.oplog.rs.stats.storageStats.freeStorageSize" then "free"
    else if r._field == "local.oplog.rs.stats.storageStats.storageSize" then "storage"
    else r._field
  }))
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)
  |> yield(name: "mean")

from(bucket: v.defaultBucket)
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "ftdc")
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r["hostname"] =~ /^${hostname:regex}$/)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r["version"] =~ /^${version:regex}$/)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field == "systemMetrics.cpu.idle_ms" or r._field == "systemMetrics.cpu.user_ms" or r._field == "systemMetrics.cpu.iowait_ms" or r._field == "systemMetrics.cpu.nice_ms" or r._field == "systemMetrics.cpu.softirq_ms" or r._field == "systemMetrics.cpu.steal_ms" or r._field == "systemMetrics.cpu.system_ms")
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: last, createEmpty: false)
  |> pivot(rowKey:["_time"], columnKey: ["_field"], valueColumn: "_value")
  |> derivative(unit: 1s, nonNegative: true, columns: ["systemMetrics.cpu.idle_ms", "systemMetrics.cpu.user_ms", "systemMetrics.cpu.iowait_ms", "systemMetrics.cpu.nice_ms", "systemMetrics.cpu.softirq_ms", "systemMetrics.cpu.steal_ms", "systemMetrics.cpu.system_ms"])
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({
      _time: r._time,
      idle_ms: r["systemMetrics.cpu.idle_ms"],
      user_ms: r["systemMetrics.cpu.user_ms"],
      iowait_ms: r["systemMetrics.cpu.iowait_ms"],
      nice_ms: r["systemMetrics.cpu.nice_ms"],
      softirq_ms: r["systemMetrics.cpu.softirq_ms"],
      steal_ms: r["systemMetrics.cpu.steal_ms"],
      system_ms: r["systemMetrics.cpu.system_ms"],
      total_ms: r["systemMetrics.cpu.idle_ms"] + r["systemMetrics.cpu.user_ms"] + r["systemMetrics.cpu.iowait_ms"] + r["systemMetrics.cpu.nice_ms"] + r["systemMetrics.cpu.softirq_ms"] + r["systemMetrics.cpu.steal_ms"] + r["systemMetrics.cpu.system_ms"]
  }))
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({
      _time: r._time,
      user: 100.0 * float(v: r.user_ms) / float(v: r.total_ms),
      system: 100.0 * float(v: r.system_ms) / float(v: r.total_ms),
      iowait: 100.0 * float(v: r.iowait_ms) / float(v: r.total_ms),
  }))

import "strings"

 |> filter(fn: (r) => r["hostname"] =~ /^${hostname:regex}$/)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r["version"] =~ /^${version:regex}$/)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field =~ /^systemMetrics\.disks\..*\.writes$/ or r._field =~ /^systemMetrics\.disks\..*\.reads$/)
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({r with _field: strings.trimPrefix(v: r._field, prefix: "systemMetrics.disks.")}))
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: last, createEmpty: false)
  |> derivative(unit: 1s)

from(bucket: v.defaultBucket)
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "ftdc")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["hostname"] =~ /^${hostname:regex}$/)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] =~ /^serverStatus\.opLatencies\.(reads|writes|commands)\.(latency|ops)$/)
  |> pivot(rowKey:["_time"], columnKey: ["_field"], valueColumn: "_value")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ _time: r._time, read_avg: if r["serverStatus.opLatencies.reads.ops"] > 0 then float(v: r["serverStatus.opLatencies.reads.latency"]) / float(v: r["serverStatus.opLatencies.reads.ops"]) else 0.0, write_avg: if r["serverStatus.opLatencies.writes.ops"] > 0 then float(v: r["serverStatus.opLatencies.writes.latency"]) / float(v: r["serverStatus.opLatencies.writes.ops"]) else 0.0, cmd_avg: if r["serverStatus.opLatencies.commands.ops"] > 0 then float(v: r["serverStatus.opLatencies.commands.latency"]) / float(v: r["serverStatus.opLatencies.commands.ops"]) else 0.0 }))
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)

from(bucket: v.defaultBucket)
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "ftdc")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["hostname"] =~ /^${hostname:regex}$/)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] =~ /serverStatus\.opLatencies\.(reads|writes|commands)\.(latency|ops)$/)
  |> pivot(rowKey:["_time"], columnKey: ["_field"], valueColumn: "_value")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({
      _time: r._time,
      read_avg: if r["serverStatus.opLatencies.reads.ops"] > 0 then float(v: r["serverStatus.opLatencies.reads.latency"]) / float(v: r["serverStatus.opLatencies.reads.ops"]) else 0.0,
      write_avg: if r["serverStatus.opLatencies.writes.ops"] > 0 then float(v: r["serverStatus.opLatencies.writes.latency"]) / float(v: r["serverStatus.opLatencies.writes.ops"]) else 0.0,
      cmd_avg: if r["serverStatus.opLatencies.commands.ops"] > 0 then float(v: r["serverStatus.opLatencies.commands.latency"]) / float(v: r["serverStatus.opLatencies.commands.ops"]) else 0.0
  }))
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)
